content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance · · · Administration · · · Executive/Legal · · · Enforcement · · · Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660        322-5660           322-5901         322-4441          322-4444

May 31, 1979

Lionel J. Wilson
Mayor
City of Oakland
City Hall
14th and Washington Streets
Oakland, CA 94612

Dear Mayor Wilson:

Thank you for your letter of May 2, 1979, regarding the conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.

Enclosed is a copy of an opinion to John Ferraro, 4 FPPC Opinions 62
(No. 78-209, Nov. 7, 1978) which concerned a rent control ordinance in
Los Angeles. In summary, the opinion states that the official who owns
three or fewer rental units may participate in rent control decisions
regardless of whether their interests will be materially affected. Officials who own more than three rental units must disqualify themselves from
any rent control decision which foreseeably would have a material financial
effect on the income value or fair market value of the rental property.

In determining the number of rental units attributable to a public
official and whether the official will be materially affected, the official
should include all rental units owned by himself, his spouse and dependent
children.

If I may be of further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact
me.

Sincerely,

Ted Prim, Chief
Conflicts of Interest Division

Enclosure
TP:ma

Wilson Mayor City of Oakland City Hall 14th and Washington Streets Oakland, CA 94612 Dear Mayor Wilson: Thank you for your letter of May 2, 1979, regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act. Enclosed is a copy of an opinion to John Ferraro, 4 FPPC Opinions 62 (No. 78-009, Nov. 7, 1978) which concerned a rent control ordinance in Los Angeles. In summary, the opinion states that the official who owns three or fewer rental units may participate in rent control decisions regardless of whether their interests will be materially affected. Officials who own more than three rental units must disqualify themselves from any rent control decision which foreseeably would have a material financial effect on the income value or fair market value of the rental property. In determining the number of rental units attributable to a public official and whether the official will be materially affected, the official should include all rental units owned by himself, his spouse and dependent children. If I may be of further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me. Sincerely, Ted Prim Chief Conflicts of Interest Division Enclosure TP:ms
